code_support_evidence.finding: FFNO bottleneck blocks already accept an explicit local convolution branch via local_conv_kernel_size.
The PDEBench CNS model builder already wires ffno_bottleneck_local_conv and ffno_bottleneck_local_conv_kernel_size into the FFNO bottleneck model.
The ffno_bottleneck_localconv_base profile is already registered with the same shell as ffno_bottleneck_base plus explicit local-conv provenance fields.
Focused generator coverage already checks that the local branch preserves shape and exposes a 3x3 Conv2d path.
Study-model coverage already checks that the local-conv profile differs only by explicit local-branch markers and still builds correctly.

non_authoritative_roots.reason: Invocation and fixed-contract metadata exist, but comparison_summary.json, comparison_summary.csv, and metrics_ffno_bottleneck_localconv_base.json are missing. Use the later 20260428T090626Z root as the authoritative 40-epoch local-conv row.

task1_conclusions.task1_time_interpretation: At Task 1 inspection time the code-level local-conv implementation was already present, the authoritative 10-epoch local-conv pilot root was available, and the 40-epoch FFNO-close fairness anchor plus authoritative 40-epoch local-conv pilot root still needed fresh execution.
task1_conclusions.readiness_only_reference_caveat: Inherited 10-epoch FFNO-close and shared-spectral anchors plus the reused 40-epoch shared-spectral anchor remain readiness-provenance historical references. The execution plan explicitly allowed them as labeled historical anchors while requiring newly generated local-conv and backfill rows to use pilot mode.
